Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn error-message
  "Given a spec named `k`, return its human-readable error message."
  [k])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/alpha_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn error-message
  "Given a spec named `k`, return its human-readable error message."
  [k]
  (reduce (fn [_ k]
            (when-let [msg (get @registry-ref k)]
              (reduced msg)))
          nil
          (util/spec-vals k)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test

(defn defmsg
  "Associates the spec named `k` with `error-message`."
  [k error-message]
  (swap! registry-ref assoc k error-message)
  nil)

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn explain-result
  "Given a result from `clojure.spec.test.alpha/check`, prints a summary of the result."
  [check-result])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/alpha_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn explain-result
  "Given a result from `clojure.spec.test.alpha/check`, prints a summary of the result."
  [check-result]
  (when (= s/*explain-out* s/explain-printer)
    (throw (ex-info "Cannot print check results with default printer. Use 'set!' or 'binding' to use Expound printer." {})))
  (s/*explain-out* check-result))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn explain-results
  "Given a sequence of results from `clojure.spec.test.alpha/check`, prints a summary of the results."
  [check-results])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/alpha_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn explain-results
  "Given a sequence of results from `clojure.spec.test.alpha/check`, prints a summary of the results."
  [check-results]
  (doseq [check-result (butlast check-results)]
    (explain-result check-result)
    (print "\n\n"))
  (explain-result (last check-results)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn pprint-str
  "Returns the pretty-printed string"
  [x])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/printer_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn pprint-str
  "Returns the pretty-printed string"
  [x]
  (if (fn? x)
    (pprint-fn x)
    (pprint/write x :stream nil)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn no-trailing-whitespace
  "Given an potentially multi-line string, returns that string with all
  trailing whitespace removed."
  [s])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/printer_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn no-trailing-whitespace
  "Given an potentially multi-line string, returns that string with all
  trailing whitespace removed."
  [s]
  (let [s' (->> s
                string/split-lines
                (map string/trimr)
                (string/join "\n"))]
    (if (= \newline (last s))
      (str s' "\n")
      s')))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn highlighted-value
  "Given a problem, returns a pretty printed
   string that highlights the problem value"
  [opts problem])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/printer_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn highlighted-value
  "Given a problem, returns a pretty printed
   string that highlights the problem value"
  [opts problem]
  (let [{:keys [:expound/form :expound/in]} problem
        {:keys [show-valid-values?] :or {show-valid-values? false}} opts
        printed-val (pprint-str (paths/value-in form in))
        relevant (str "(" :expound.problems/relevant "|(" :expound.problems/kv-relevant "\\s+" :expound.problems/kv-relevant "))")
        regex (re-pattern (str "(.*)" relevant ".*"))
        s (binding [*print-namespace-maps* false]
            (if (:show-valid-values? opts)
              (pprint-str (summary-form show-valid-values? form in))
              (pprint-str (walk/prewalk-replace {:expound.problems/irrelevant '...} (summary-form show-valid-values? form in)))))
        [line prefix & _more] (re-find regex s)]
    (if-not line ;; can be nil depending on unforeseen *print-length* / *print-level* values:
      (str
       printed-val
       "\n\nin\n\n"
       (pprint-str form))
      (let [highlighted-line (-> line
                                 (string/replace (re-pattern relevant) (escape-replacement
                                                                        (re-pattern relevant)
                                                                        (indent 0 (count prefix) (ansi/color printed-val :bad-value))))
                                 (str "\n" (ansi/color (highlight-line prefix printed-val)
                                                       :pointer)))]
        ;;highlighted-line
        (no-trailing-whitespace (string/replace s line (escape-replacement line highlighted-line)))))))

Contract type: test_contract

(defn compare-paths [path1 path2]
  (->> (map compare-path-segment path1 path2)
       (remove #{0})
       first))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn value-in
  "Similar to get-in, but works with paths that reference map keys"
  [form in])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: test/expound/paths_test.cljc

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn value-in
  "Similar to get-in, but works with paths that reference map keys"
  [form in]
  (if (nil? in)
    form
    (let [[k & rst] in]
      (cond
        (empty? in)
        form

        (and (map? form) (kps? k))
        (recur (:key k) rst)

        (and (map? form) (kvps? k))
        (recur (nth (seq form) (:idx k)) rst)

        (associative? form)
        (recur (get form k) rst)

        (and (int? k)
             (seqable? form))
        (recur (nth (seq form) k) rst)

        :else
        (throw (ex-info "No value found"
                        {:form form
                         :in in}))))))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn esc
  "Returns an ANSI escope string which will apply the given collection of SGR
  codes."
  [codes])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn esc
  "Returns an ANSI escope string which will apply the given collection of SGR
  codes."
  [codes]
  (let [codes (map sgr-code codes codes)
        codes (string/join \; codes)]
    (str \u001b \[ codes \m)))

(defn sgr
  "Wraps the given string with SGR escapes to apply the given codes, then reset
